description: Fix missing timeout and error handling in the E2E test plugin's failing-tool, which uses raw fetch() instead of testApi(), inconsistent with all other tools in the plugin.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Replace raw fetch with testApi in failing-tool handler
userStories.description: As a developer running E2E tests, I want failing-tool to use testApi() like every other tool in the plugin so that it has consistent timeout handling and structured error responses instead of potentially hanging indefinitely or throwing unstructured errors.
acceptanceCriteria: failing-tool handle() uses testApi() from '../test-api.js' instead of raw fetch()
The tool still throws a ToolError with the error_code and error_message from the server response (same observable behavior as before)
The tool benefits from testApi's 30s timeout and structured error handling for network failures
No unused imports remain (fetch, Response, etc. should be removed if no longer needed)
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run build passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: plugins/e2e-test/src/tools/failing-tool.ts (lines 24-53).

The bug: This tool uses raw `fetch('/api/fail', ...)` without:
1. A timeout signal (all other tools use testApi which has `AbortSignal.timeout(30_000)`)
2. Proper error handling for network failures (if fetch throws, the error is unstructured)
3. Error handling for non-JSON responses (if `res.json()` fails, the error is unstructured)

Every other tool in the E2E plugin uses `testApi()` from `../test-api.js`. This tool bypasses it because it needs to inspect the error response body before throwing. But `testApi()` already does exactly this — it reads the response, checks `ok: false`, extracts `error` and `error_code`, and throws a ToolError.

Fix: Replace the raw fetch with testApi(). The handler should call:
```typescript
await testApi('/api/fail', {
  error_code: params.error_code ?? 'deliberate_failure',
  error_message: params.error_message ?? 'This tool always fails',
});
```

testApi() will throw a ToolError when the server returns `{ ok: false, error: '...', error_code: '...' }`. The handler doesn't need to manually parse the response.

Note: The `output` schema says `{ ok: z.boolean() }` but the tool always throws and never returns. This is fine — the output schema describes what would be returned on success, which never happens for this tool. Do NOT change the output schema.

Also remove the import of `ToolError` if it becomes unused after the refactor (testApi handles the error throwing). Actually, check if ToolError is still imported — if testApi handles everything, the direct ToolError import may no longer be needed. But keep it if it's still used.
